text:
Once upon an antique moon, there lived a wise old owl named Oddweise. He had feathers of midnight blue and eyes as deep as oceans. Every night, Oddweise flew south to his favourite haunt—ancient oak woods near the grand cathedral.

One particular moonlit evening, Oddweise heard a distant singing. It sounded like birds in perfect harmony. He followed the melody. The trees parted like red velvet. At the forest's edge stood a statue of a lady guardian. She smiled.

Oddweise felt warmer. He befriended her every night.

Then one day, owl books weren. Owls speak.

"Why won't they see me?"

The guardian bowed.

"It's your secret."

They whispered tales.

Oddweise flew high.

Stars heard.

They listened.

"What makes us special."

Their wings beat.

"We listen."

One moon, Oddweise flew home.

Stars smiled.

Now, every night.

He finds her.

The owl loved her. She loves him.

They met.<|endoftext|>
